item.title: Japan Adds to Steel Scrutiny With Probes on China, Taiwan, Korea
item.description: Japan has launched an anti-dumping probe into imports of key steel products shipped from China, South Korea and Taiwan, adding to signs of trade stress in a sector suffering from global overcapacity.

item.title: Hiab Agrees to Acquire Truck Maker Labrie for $1.04 Billion
item.description: Finnish-listed Hiab Oyj has agreed to acquire refuse collection vehicle manufacturer Labrie Environmental Group at an enterprise value of $1.04 billion, strengthening its position in the North American waste and recycling market.

item.title: EM Stocks Hit Record High on AI, Currencies Lower as Oil Climbs
item.description: Emerging Asian equities rose to a record, propelled by continuing enthusiasm for the artificial intelligence trade that’s bolstered global stocks despite the Iran war.

item.title: India Stock Trading Value Hits Record as MSCI Changes Spur Deals
item.description: Trading value in India’s cash equities surged to an all-time high Friday, as MSCI Inc.’s index changes triggered a flurry of bulk deals by global passive funds.

item.title: China Tightens Outbound Investment Rules With Eye on Security
item.description: China strengthened oversight of outbound investment through a new directive, tightening cross-border capital flows as its technology rivalry with the US intensifies.

item.title: Nvidia Enters Windows Laptop Market, Taking On Intel and AMD
item.description: Nvidia Corp. is entering the PC market with a new chip aimed at loosening the stranglehold of Intel Corp. technology in that arena and modernizing the machines for the AI era.

item.title: Nvidia Says Anthropic, OpenAI Among Users of New Vera Chip
item.description: Nvidia Corp. said Anthropic PBC, OpenAI and SpaceX are among the first big users of its upcoming microprocessor, securing key customers for its latest attempt to expand an already extensive footprint in AI data centers.

item.title: AI Savings Misses ‘Should Be Making Executives Uncomfortable,’ Bain Says
item.description: Cost savings from automation are broadly falling short of projections, according to a new Bain & Co. global survey of large companies.

item.title: A Strategic Debacle, But Stocks Keep Winning
item.description: A lot is riding on whether inflation from the Iran war can stay manageable.

item.title: Copper Gains With US Tariffs Deadline Less Than a Month Away
item.description: Copper advanced in New York and London to kick off a crucial month that should deliver more clarity on the Trump administration’s plans for levies on imports of the metal to the US.

item.title: Aussie Risks More Losses Versus Kiwi on Rate View, Analysts Say
item.description: The Australian dollar may have further scope to fall against the kiwi as traders unwind bearish positions in New Zealand’s currency following a hawkish shift from the nation’s central bank, analysts said.

item.title: LG Electronics Shares Jump Over 300% in 2026 on Physical AI Push
item.description: LG Electronics Inc. shares have quadrupled this year as investors applaud the venerable Korean appliance maker’s shift into robotics.

item.title: India Keeps Local Jet Fuel Prices Unchanged After Airline Plea
item.description: India’s state-owned oil refiners have kept jet fuel prices steady for local flights following a plea from airlines to hold off on further hikes as they grapple with the fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: アップルが次に狙うは眼鏡市場、スマートグラスで既存業界を切り崩せるか－Power On
item.description: Apple Watchで腕時計業界を塗り替えたアップルが、スマートフォン市場に匹敵する巨大な眼鏡市場の開拓に取り組んでいる

item.title: SMBC Nikko Mulls Launching $627 Million Japan Mezzanine Fund
item.description: SMBC Nikko Securities Inc. is considering establishing a fund with One Investment Management to provide so-called mezzanine financing, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Korea Boosts Bond-Market Vigil Via Daily Phone Calls, Group Chat
item.description: South Korea intensified monitoring of the government bond market through daily phone calls and a private messaging group with market participants, as authorities step up efforts to contain rising yields.

item.title: ECB’s Schnabel Sees Risk of Unanchored Inflation Views From War
item.description: The European Central Bank can no longer look through the inflationary impact of the war in Iran as price pressures spread beyond energy and the risk of unanchored inflation expectations rises, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

item.title: Weak Monsoon Forecast Adds to India’s Inflation, Growth Worries
item.description: The biggest risk is to rural consumption, a key driver of economic growth.

item.title: SoftBank Set to Dethrone Toyota as Japan’s Most Valuable Company
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is poised to overtake Toyota Motor Corp. as Japan’s most valuable company, marking a milestone for the global artificial intelligence boom and a dramatic reshuffling of the country’s corporate hierarchy.

item.title: Top Tech Fund Plans to Buy SK Hynix in Bet on Memory Chip Crunch
item.description: A top-performing technology fund plans to own SK Hynix Inc.’s shares, betting that tighter supply will further benefit the South Korean AI memory chipmaker after a 1,000% rally in the past year.

item.title: Yen Defies Record Intervention as Wait for BOJ Hike Raises Risks
item.description: Yen traders face a heightened risk of intervention over the next two weeks after Japan’s currency defied historic attempts to prop it up.

item.title: Ryan Is Said to Agree to Buy Nordic Tax Adviser Svalner Atlas
item.description: Ryan LLC, backed by Neuberger and Onex Corp., has agreed to acquire European tax adviser Svalner Atlas Advisors, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Congo’s Ebola Footprint Widens as Officials Race to Gauge Epidemic’s True Scale
item.description: Congo’s Ebola outbreak is being detected across a widening swath of the country’s conflict-hit east as health authorities struggle to trace exposed contacts and determine the true scale of the epidemic.

item.title: Goldman Sees Two-Sided Risk to Oil as Lower Use Offsets Iran War
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. sees two-sided risks to oil prices as a slump in demand competes with supply losses from the Middle East because of the Iran war.

item.title: China’s Private Factory Gauge Slows as Economy Softens
item.description: China’s manufacturing activity slowed in May, according to a private survey of export-oriented firms, in line with an official gauge that suggests the world’s second-largest economy is losing some steam.

item.title: India Rate Risks, Fiscal Worries Add to Pressure on Bond Yields
item.description: The recent surge in Indian bond yields may extend as investors weigh prospects of interest-rate hikes, while concerns grow over the government’s fiscal position.

item.title: Gen-Z Gamer’s 3D-Model Startup Becomes China’s Latest AI Unicorn
item.description: Vast, a 3D-modeling startup founded by a 29-year-old gamer, raised nearly $200 million to become the latest Chinese AI outfit to achieve a valuation of $1 billion.

item.title: Powell Says Fed Credibility Lost If President Can Fire Officials
item.description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said the US central bank would lose the credibility that’s required to support a strong and stable economy if any president were free to dismiss Fed officials over policy disagreements.

item.title: China Investors Turn Sellers of HK Stocks in Rare Reversal
item.description: Chinese mainland investors turned net sellers of Hong Kong stocks for the first time in nearly three years in May, after a surge in tech shares trading onshore eroded the appeal of the city’s market.

item.title: Best ECB Response to Stablecoins Is Digital Euro, Schnabel Says
item.description: Stablecoins pose a number of risks to financial stability and monetary policy, and the European Central Bank’s best response is to ensure that public money remains the anchor of the system, according to Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.
